questions: What is the smallest useful Inquisition v0.1 organ contract that guarantees no-fake-green audit capability?
Which audit categories must Inquisition v0.1 detect before READY_FOR_AGENT can be set true for any task?
What should Inquisition v0.1 explicitly NOT audit yet to avoid scope creep?
Which files are mandatory for a real organ (minimum viable organ structure)?
Which schemas are missing that Inquisition v0.1 needs?
Which checks prevent fake green most effectively with minimal implementation?
What could cause unsafe over-automation if Inquisition is given too much authority?
Which stage in the blueprint task should be executed first and why?
What conditions should block READY_FOR_AGENT for the blueprint task specifically?
How should Inquisition report UNKNOWN status without turning it into a false PASS?
constraints: Advisory is not doctrine until reconciled
No READY_FOR_AGENT without explicit evidence and owner approval
Blueprint task is planning only — no implementation
Advice is raw input until ingested and reconciled through modernization
required_response_format: Use ORGANS/ASTRONOMICON/REGISTRY/ADVISORY_RESPONSES/* with schema advisory_response.schema.json. Map accepted/rejected recommendations explicitly. Include risk assessment for each recommendation.
no_fake_green_rules: Do not declare READY_FOR_AGENT in review response
Provide blockers instead of optimistic PASS claims
Do not claim implementation exists based on blueprint alone
Do not treat this review pack as proof of Inquisition readiness
